title: Ways to Describe Emotions - ANGER
description: Comprehensive guide to understanding anger emotions, their triggers, manifestations, and effects

promptingEvents.description: Events that trigger feelings of anger
events: You or someone you care about being attacked or threatened by others
Not having things turn out as expected

interpretations.description: Interpretations of events that prompt feelings of anger
interpretations: Judging that the situation is illegitimate or wrong
Ruminating about the event that set off the anger in the first place
Believing that you have been treated unfairly
Believing that important goals are being blocked or stopped
Believing that things 'should' be different than they are

biologicalChanges.description: Biological changes and experiences of anger
changes: Feeling your face flush or get hot
Feeling like you are going to explode
Wanting to hit someone, bang the wall, throw something, blow up

behaviors: Using a loud, quarrelsome, or sarcastic voice

effects: Attending only to the situation that's making you angry
Ruminating about the situation making you angry or about situations in the past
Imagining future situations that will make you angry
